reason: Both answers correctly state exactly-once delivery is impossible (via a Two Generals/ack-ambiguity argument), correctly distinguish delivery from processing, and land on the same standard approach: at-least-once delivery + idempotent processing via dedup keys and transactional/outbox patterns, with the same caveat about Kafka/Flink-style EOS being scoped to their own systems. Both are concise and actionable. Answer A adds a concrete idempotent-operation example (upsert vs increment) and a crisp 'what to tell your teammate' takeaway; Answer B is slightly more structured (numbered list) and adds SQS FIFO as another example of the pattern. Neither has factual errors, and neither pads unnecessarily. The two are comparable in correctness and usefulness, with different minor strengths that roughly cancel out.
